jd_text: Transition Management Analyst, Assistant Vice President Who We Are Looking For We are seeking candidates with 3+ years of experience to join our Transition Management (TM) team in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. In this role, you’ll work within State Street Investment Management’s global TM business, delivering transition and interim exposure management solutions to institutional investors worldwide. You’ll support and manage complex transitions across multiple asset classes, including equities, fixed income, FX, and exchange‑traded derivatives, serving clients domiciled in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia while contributing to the firm’s broader global transition management platform, including interim exposure management and reporting and analytics. This role can be performed in a hybrid model, requiring at least 4 days of work in the office. Why this role is important to us State Street Investment Management (SSIM) is a global asset manager partnering with some of the world’s most sophisticated investors. Backed by State Street’s scale and stability, SSIM offers employees the opportunity to work on complex, high‑impact investment solutions in a collaborative, forward‑looking environment—where expertise, partnership, and long‑term thinking truly matter This role will enable local delivery of our industry-leading TM service to clients domiciled in the KSA, and enhance our global coverage model by adding TM analysts in the Middle East. Expanding our global presence aligns with our continued commitment to providing the highest level of service to our clients. What You Will Be Responsible For As a Transition Management Analyst, you will: Gain an in-depth understanding of the transition life cycle including pre-trade, implementation, and post-trade aspects. Support KSA-domiciled clients and our global transition desk with pre- and post-trade analysis and execution reporting; planning and project management including communication across parties (clients, custodians, record-keepers, investment managers and consultants); portfolio management and order generation; collaboration with trading desk on trade strategy and instructions; and monitoring of daily middle office support functions (trade communications, reconciliations, corporate actions). Conduct trade reconciliation between internal systems for accuracy and completeness prior to sending to middle office and custodians to ensure timely settlement of trades. Develop expertise in multi-asset agency trading, exposure management, and hedging strategies. Represent transition and interim exposure management products as a subject matter expert. Become an expert on benchmarking and measurement of our transition and trade execution performance through Transaction Cost Analysis (TCA) Support sales and business development initiatives and contribute to thought leadership on transition, exposure management and trading topics Contribute to projects to evolve and industrialize our suite of trading, hedging and exposure analysis tools – including ongoing enhancement of pre- and post-trade analytics and interim exposure management platform – working with our Exposure Solutions team, reporting and analytics specialists, and external vendors What We Value The following skills will help you succeed in this role: Portfolio management, trading and/or operational experience, preferably across multiple asset classes Familiarity with Charles River Development, Axioma, or other portfolio and order management systems / tools Technical proficiency, with experience using Bloomberg and other market-data sources for analytical purposes Strong verbal, written and interpersonal communication skills; ability to clearly articulate complex topics and navigate projects with clients and internal stakeholders Ability to work and problem solve independently under tight deadlines in a fast-paced environment, all while maintaining a critical focus on details Strong project management and organizational skills; pro-active in seeking out the next project to help the team Education & Preferred Qualifications 3+ years of experience in a portfolio management, trading, consulting, analytical or operational role Additional Requirements CMA registration required Willingness to travel up to 10% of the time Bachelor’s degree in engineering, mathematics, economics, finance or similar MBA, graduate degree in a quantitative discipline, or progress toward CFA designation preferred Experience with TCA, AI, programming languages, modeling in Excel / VBA and Python, data visualization software, and database infrastructures a plus About State Street Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success. We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law.
